body: ``` rust-web-box — in-browser Rust sandbox Powered by CheerpX (leaningtech/webvm) and VS Code Web. [rust-web-box] Booting Linux VM… ............ [rust-web-box] Linux VM ready ✓ [rust-web-box] disk: ./disk/rust-alpine.ext2 [rust-web-box] Workspace mirrored to /workspace — try `cargo run`. root@rust-web-box:/workspace# cargo run Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized] target(s) in 32.81s Running `target/debug/hello` Hello from rust-web-box! This binary was compiled inside CheerpX. root@rust-web-box:/workspace# cargo run Compiling hello v0.1.0 (/workspace) Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized] target(s) in 8m 19s Running `target/debug/hello` Hello from rust-web-box! 2 Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX. root@rust-web-box:/workspace# cargo run Compiling hello v0.1.0 (/workspace) Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized] target(s) in 2.07s Running `target/debug/hello` Hello from rust-web-box! 3 Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX. root@rust-web-box:/workspace# cargo run Compiling hello v0.1.0 (/workspace) Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized] target(s) in 2.02s Running `target/debug/hello` Hello from rust-web-box! 5 Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX. root@rust-web-box:/workspace# ``` At the moment I see `Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX.`, `This binary was compiled inside CheerpX.`, that looks, like something we have added in our code. Double check we don't faking the build, but actually using real `cargo run` commands, and they are really executed in the browser. And we don't need such output to each command, but just use real bash command. If we have any fake solutions we must delete them, and do only real as simple as possible solutions that will just work 1 to 1 to real deal, so it should behave as real terminal with real linux distribution installed. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Also double check to fully apply requirements to entire codebase, so if we have issue in multiple places, it should be fixed in all them. Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Double check the solution is not fake.
